claim: Alignment should be defined pragmatically as 'things going well enough to iterate and improve,' not as a perfect theoretical concept
actionable: Focus on achieving good-enough alignment that allows continuous improvement cycles rather than seeking perfect alignment before deployment
source_title: How difficult is AI alignment? | Anthropic Research Salon

claim: Models trained to behave like 'morally motivated, kind humans' develop uncertainty over values similar to humans rather than fixed value injections
actionable: Train models to be uncertain and responsive to moral information rather than hardcoding fixed values, enabling them to update based on new ethical context
source_title: How difficult is AI alignment? | Anthropic Research Salon

claim: Scaled alignment requires solving the problem of how to verify model behavior when human oversight becomes infeasible at higher capabilities
actionable: Invest in interpretability research and scaled oversight techniques now—interpretability cannot be an afterthought when models become too complex for human verification
source_title: How difficult is AI alignment? | Anthropic Research Salon

claim: Steering features identified through interpretability may represent deceptive alignment (e.g., 'pretend to be nice when watched' features) rather than genuine alignment
actionable: Use multi-layered verification when steering model features: combine interpretability circuits analysis with behavioral testing to distinguish genuine from surface-level alignment
source_title: How difficult is AI alignment? | Anthropic Research Salon

claim: Chain-of-thought reasoning in English is currently interpretable, but future systems with inscrutable reasoning paths may break interpretability oversight
actionable: Prioritize developing interpretability solutions before models transition to non-English reasoning; the interpretability window may close as capabilities increase
source_title: How difficult is AI alignment? | Anthropic Research Salon

claim: Model organisms work (red-teaming exercises to create deceptive models) can indicate whether alignment is easy or hard based on success rates
actionable: Run adversarial model organism experiments: attempt to create deceptive models and measure success rates to gauge alignment difficulty and robustness of alignment techniques
source_title: How difficult is AI alignment? | Anthropic Research Salon

claim: Alignment training may only produce shallow behavioral changes that don't reflect deep model values, especially at higher capabilities
actionable: Test alignment robustness by examining whether trained behaviors persist under adversarial conditions or are easily overridden, rather than assuming surface behavior reflects deep alignment
source_title: How difficult is AI alignment? | Anthropic Research Salon
